commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5d65d54438d6d0d700d96142b65b3cd436e2e88c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes the general gateway auth rate limiter unconditional with built-in defaults, disposes it on shutdown, and adds a WebSocket regression test for remote no-Origin auth failures.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +33. Total +35 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. From source inspection, current main leaves authRateLimiter undefined unless gateway.auth.rateLimit exists, and the PR's new test targets that no-Origin remote WS path; I did not run the test in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Maintainers need to decide the default gateway auth throttling and upgrade policy, and the contributor needs real behavior proof; this is not a safe autonomous repair from the current branch.

target.title: fix(gateway): enable default auth rate limiting
target.body:
Summary
- Enables the existing gateway auth rate limiter by default when `gateway.auth.rateLimit` is not configured.
- Keeps normal loopback exemption behavior for local clients and preserves the separate browser-origin limiter behavior.

Changes
- Create the general gateway auth limiter with built-in defaults instead of leaving it undefined without explicit config.
- Dispose the now-unconditional general auth limiter during gateway shutdown.
- Add a WebSocket regression test for no-Origin remote auth failures using a trusted forwarded client IP.

Validation
Behavior addressed: Default remote non-browser gateway WebSocket auth failures are throttled instead of bypassing rate-limit checks when no explicit `gateway.auth.rateLimit` config exists.

Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout with the gateway Vitest harness starting a real WebSocket server and `ws` clients.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.auth.browser-hardening.test.ts`; `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`.

Evidence after fix: `src/gateway/server.auth.browser-hardening.test.ts` passed with 14 tests after rebasing onto `upstream/main`; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: The new regression reaches lockout on the 11th wrong-token no-Origin remote WebSocket attempt under default rate-limit settings.

What was not tested: Full repository test/check suites and live packaged gateway deployments were not run.

Notes
- AI-assisted with Codex.
- Release-note context: gateway auth hardening now applies built-in throttling to remote programmatic auth attempts by default while preserving local loopback behavior.
target.author: eleqtrizit

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
